name: Beef Stew with Cranberries
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Stewing beef (or lamb or pork cut into 1-in cubes)
3/4 cup Beef stock or beef broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 tablespoon each of oil and butter in a 6-quart pot.
When the oil is hot, add half the beef and brown it well on all sides. Remove and reserve the meat.
2. Heat the remaining oil and butter. Add the rest of the beef and brown it well. Remove the pot from heat and discard any fat. Return the meat to the pot and season with 1/2 teaspoon salt and freshly ground pepper.
3. Using a metal blade, process the garlic through the feed tube until minced. Add the minced garlic to the pot.
4. Using a medium shredding disk, shred the onions. Use a slotted spoon to lift the onions from their juice and add them to the pot. Discard any remaining juice.
5. Add the red wine, beef stock or broth, vinegar, and tomato paste to the pot. Return to heat and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover, and cook until the meat is tender (about 2 hours).
7. Using a metal blade, pulse the cranberries, brown sugar, and flour in the pot for about 10 seconds. Add the chopped mixture to the stew and stir well.
8. Adjust seasoning as needed. The stew can be served immediately or refrigerated and gently reheated up to 4 days later. It can also be frozen for up to 3 months.
